behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between summary-write logic and persisted per-dimension findings for the same round (module/data handoff), not a single pure function. In-process DB/repository wiring is enough to reproduce it. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is about stored verdict reconciliation, not model-output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Set up review findings in a local test DB for round R with at least one FAIL dimension. Attempt to store a PASS summary for round R through the real service/repository path. Assert write is rejected with a policy error and no PASS summary is persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module correctness check: gate computation must source truth from stored per-dimension findings, not summary text. Needs repository + gate wiring, but no real external infra. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Persist findings for a round with one MUST-FIX/FAIL dimension, plus a contradictory hand-written PASS summary. Run stop-gate computation via production gate service. Assert final verdict is non-PASS and matches stored findings, proving summary text is not trusted as sole input.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans input validation, override authorization flag handling, and audit-record persistence. Real failure appears when these modules interact. Rejected System: auditable warning can be validated with local persistence/logger sink; no real external infra required by text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call summary-finalization endpoint/service twice: (1) manual override text without override flag; assert rejection and no override applied. (2) same input with explicit override flag; assert accepted and an audit warning record persisted with actor, round, timestamp, and reason.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly anchors gating to commit chronology ("latest fix commit") and round IDs generated after that point. Real failures often occur at VCS/subprocess boundary (e.g., stale HEAD/diff base) and can be hidden by in-process fakes. Rejected Workflow: text requires temporal ordering between fix commit and one post-fix review round, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo fixture, create commit A (pre-fix), run/save review round RA, then create fix commit B. Attempt PASS using RA and assert block. Run a new review round RB generated after B, then assert PASS can proceed. Use real git state/subprocess-backed commit resolution used by the gate.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires a real review-agent round in the live loop; execution path includes a real model call. A stubbed agent can mask failures where stale/non-fresh agent execution is treated as fresh. Rejected Workflow: this behavior requires freshness of a single post-update agent round, not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end live loop on a changed diff. Try to finalize PASS without triggering a new real review-agent run after the diff update; assert block. Then execute a real fresh agent round on updated diff and assert gate allows progression (subject to findings).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is policy evaluation over stored findings + round-budget state, typically across repository and gate-policy modules. In-process wiring suffices. Rejected System: no required real OS/network/subprocess from behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: depends on stored verdict states, not real-time model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local persistence with unresolved MUST-FIX findings and mark remaining rounds = 0. Invoke gate outcome computation via real service stack. Assert outcome is ESCALATION and PASS is impossible.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets a live multi-round workflow; correctness depends on sequenced round state transitions and final gating/escalation emission across rounds. Single-step tests miss cross-round carryover and exhaustion behavior. Rejected Agentic: text requires multi-round pipeline behavior (more than one agentic step), not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live multi-round review pipeline: run at least two rounds where MUST-FIX remains unresolved, consume round budget, then attempt finalization. Assert PASS is blocked and escalation event/record is emitted at exhaustion in the same workflow run.
